title: Method of identifying gesture event types on a textile touch pad sensor
abstract: It is disclosed a method of identifying gesture event types on a textile touch pad sensor (10), the textile touch pad sensor (10) comprising a plurality of non- conductive yarns interwoven with conductive yarns forming a conductive grid (17), comprising the steps of estimating the type of a gesture event performed and when necessary correcting the estimation of the gesture type.
claims:
Claims 1 . A method of identifying gesture event types on a textile touch pad sensor (10), the textile touch pad sensor (10) comprising a plurality of non- conductive yarns interwoven with conductive yarns forming a conductive grid (17), the method comprising the steps of: memorize raw touch data generated by the conductive grid (17) during a predefined interval of time in which a gesture event is performed on the textile touch pad sensor (10); estimating the type of a gesture event performed on the textile touch pad (10) sensor by analyzing the raw touch data generated by the conductive grid (17); measuring the total time (eventLenght) of the gesture event performed on the textile touch pad sensor (10); memorizing the longest time (longestX, longestY) during which a conductive yarn of the conductive grid (17) has been touched during the gesture event; correcting the estimation of the gesture type, if the longest time (longestX, longestY) during which a conductive yarn of the conductive grid (17) has been touched is greater than a quantity that is a function of the total time (eventLenght) of the gesture event. 2. The method according to claim 1 , wherein the estimated gesture type is corrected into a gesture type having a horizontal direction component if a predetermined condition is met. 3. The method according to claim 2, wherein the predetermined condition to correct the estimated gesture type into a gesture type having an horizontal direction component is expressed by the equation: longestX > ki * eventl_ength/k2 where longestX \s the longest time during which an horizontal conductive yarn of the conductive grid (17) has been touched, eventLength is the total time of the gesture, and ki and are constant parameters. 4. The method according to claim 1 , wherein the estimated gesture type is 

 corrected into a gesture type having a vertical direction component if a predetermined condition is met. 5. The method according to claim 2, wherein the predetermined condition to correct the estimated gesture type into a gesture type having a vertical direction component is expressed by the equation: longestY > ki * eventl_ength/k2 where longestY is the longest time during which a vertical conductive yarn of the conductive grid (17) has been touched, eventLength is the total time of the gesture, and ki and are constant parameters. 6. The method according to claim 3 and 5, wherein the estimated gesture type is corrected into a single tap gesture type if the following predetermined conditions longestX > ki * eventl_ength/k2 and longestY > ki * eventl_ength/k2 are concurrently met. 7. The method according to claims 3, 5 or 6, wherein the values of ki and are determined by a calibration procedure. 8. A textile substrate for interpreting human gestures, the textile substrate comprising a textile touch pad sensor (10) comprising a plurality of non- conductive yarns interwoven with conductive yarns forming a conductive grid (17), the conductive grid (17) being connected to an electronic control unit (450) configured to receive and interpret a data structure generated by a human gesture according to the method of claims 1 to 7. 9. A computer-implemented data structure codifying a gesture performed on a textile substrate, the textile substrate comprising a textile touch pad sensor (10), the data structure comprising raw touch data generated by the conductive grid (17) during a predefined interval of time in which a gesture event is performed on the textile touch pad sensor (10), the data structure being suitable to be interpreted by an electronic control unit (450) according to the method of claims 1 to 7.
